weather.summary: Saturday: 11°C, few clouds. Sunday: 12°C, clear sky

metadata.greeting: This weekend is looking fantastic for outdoor adventures! I've found some exciting activities right in Hackney and East London, plus some awesome days out further afield. Get ready for a weekend of fun, exploration and maybe a little bit of mud!

activities.title: Library Club at the Hackney Showroom
activities.description: Reading corner and diverse book-pairing service.
activities.whyItWorks: A relaxed space for toddlers to explore books and stories, and it's free!

transport.fromHackney: Buses and Overground to Hackney Downs or Hackney Central.
transport.tip: Check term times before you go!

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: Two floors of immersive play space known as the Story Worlds as well as the Story Garden playground. The museum hosts Baby Storytelling plus crafts and singalong time for babies and toddlers.
activities.whyItWorks: Immersive play spaces and storytelling sessions designed specifically for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Stratford.
transport.tip: Check the website for specific session times and age recommendations.

activities.title: Young V&A (Museum of Childhood)
activities.description: Interactive galleries specifically designed for children, featuring toys, design and play spaces. Mini Museum for under 2s.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed with children in mind, plenty to keep them entertained, Mini Museum caters specifically to toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Bethnal Green.
transport.tip: Avoid peak hours for a less crowded experience.

activities.title: Spitalfields City Farm
activities.description: A small city farm with animals, gardens and a cafe.
activities.whyItWorks: Toddlers love animals! A chance to see and interact with farm animals in an urban setting.

transport.fromHackney: Buses to Brick Lane or Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then walk.
transport.tip: Check for special events or feeding times.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: A farm in the heart of Hackney with animals, gardens, and a cafe.
activities.whyItWorks: Easy to reach, free, and filled with animals that toddlers adore.

transport.fromHackney: Various buses stop near the farm.
transport.tip: Check their website for workshops.

activities.title: The Line Sculpture Walk
activities.venue: Various locations along the River Lea
activities.description: An outdoor art walk following the River Lea, featuring sculptures.
activities.whyItWorks: A chance to be outdoors and see interesting art. Good for short bursts of activity.

transport.fromHackney: Various buses and trains to access different points along the route.
transport.tip: Plan a short section of the walk to avoid tiring little legs.

activities.title: East Side Adventure Playground
activities.description: Adventure playground with structures, sand and water play.
activities.whyItWorks: Space to run, climb, and be adventurous! Great for burning energy.

transport.fromHackney: Various buses and walking.
transport.tip: Check opening times.

activities.title: Baby Shark's Easter Splash
activities.description: Interactive immersive experience featuring an Easter Egg Hunt, along with the characters from the Baby Shark universe.
activities.whyItWorks: Features an interactive Easter Egg Hunt, along with the characters from the Baby Shark universe.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Tottenham Court Road
transport.tip: Show features a 20-minute looping journey

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Dinosaurs, animals, and interactive exhibits. A classic London museum!
activities.whyItWorks: Dinosaurs are always a hit! Plenty of space to explore and free entry.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Liverpool Street, then Central Line to Holborn, then Piccadilly Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid crowds.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Interactive exhibits exploring science and technology. The Garden is designed for young children.
activities.whyItWorks: The Garden (basement) is an interactive space where children can discover construction, water, light and sound through play. The Pattern Pod is great for light exploration, sensory and interactive fun.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Liverpool Street, then Central Line to Holborn, then Piccadilly Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: The Garden is open Tues - Sunday.

activities.title: V&A Museum
activities.description: World-leading museum of art, design and performance. Backpacks available with activities.
activities.whyItWorks: A digital trail takes you on a journey through V&A galleries. Backpacks available with activities.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Liverpool Street, then Central Line to Holborn, then Piccadilly Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Check for family events.

activities.title: British Museum
activities.description: World-renowned museum of history and culture. Family activity backpacks available.
activities.whyItWorks: Museum Missions, Museum explorer trails and Backpacks

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Euston/King's Cross, then Northern Line to Tottenham Court Road.
transport.tip: Book in advance.

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: Small free zoo with a variety of animals including wallabies and lemurs.
activities.whyItWorks: Free entry and a chance to see animals in a park setting.

transport.fromHackney: Northern Line to Golders Green, then a short bus ride or walk.
transport.tip: Combine with a visit to Hampstead Heath.

activities.title: Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.description: Museum with natural history, anthropology, and musical instrument collections, plus gardens and an aquarium.
activities.whyItWorks: Compact museum that's perfectly sized for kids to enjoy, Animal Walk features alpacas, sheep, goats, chickens and more, plus gardens for picnics

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Forest Hill.
transport.tip: Visit the Animal Walk for free animal encounters.

activities.title: London Transport Museum
activities.description: Discover the history of London's transport system. All Aboard play area, suitable for 0 – 7 with a fleet of mini vehicles of trains
activities.whyItWorks: All Aboard play area, suitable for 0 – 7 with a fleet of mini vehicles of trains

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Euston/King's Cross, then Piccadilly Line to Covent Garden.
transport.tip: Check for sing and story time with in-house educators

activities.title: Diana Memorial Playground
activities.description: A pirate ship wooden playground with sand, water and sensory play areas.
activities.whyItWorks: Toddlers love the pirate ship and the sand and water play.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Notting Hill Gate or Bayswater, then walk through Kensington Gardens.
transport.tip: Can get busy, so arrive early.

activities.title: Spring Family Fun at Southbank Centre
activities.description: A festival of fun and creativity for families including shows, musical adventures, puppetry and free activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of variety, something for every toddler. Spring Family Fun includes super shows, musical adventures, amazing puppetry and lots of free fun in the Easter break.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Oxford Circus, then Bakerloo Line to Waterloo.
transport.tip: Check the schedule and book in advance for popular events.

activities.title: REPLAY: A Limitless Recycled Playground
activities.description: A playground made out of repurposed materials.
activities.whyItWorks: Creative and engaging play space made from recycled materials.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Oxford Circus, then Bakerloo Line to Waterloo.
transport.tip: Book in advance.

activities.title: Easter Eggsplorers at Hobbledown Heath
activities.description: Easter event with a full-scale Easter event.
activities.whyItWorks: Easter-themed fun with animals and play areas.

transport.fromHackney: Tube and train to Hounslow West, then bus.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: The Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: Family-friendly trail through the palace gardens to find hidden Lindt Gold Bunny statues.
activities.whyItWorks: An Easter trail that actually motivates kids to walk. You'll explore the palace grounds looking for gold bunny statues, finishing with a chocolate treat.

transport.fromHackney: Train from Waterloo to Hampton Court.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, especially for weekends.

activities.title: London Zoo's Zoonormous Egg Hunt
activities.description: Easter trail to discover eggs from various animal species.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines an Easter trail with fun facts about egg-laying animals.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Camden Town or Regent's Park.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance online.

activities.title: Family Fun at the Cutty Sark
activities.description: Easter Egg Trail throughout the historic ship
activities.whyItWorks: Discover fascinating facts about Cutty Sark's epic voyages, while you explore the decks

transport.fromHackney: DLR to Cutty Sark.
transport.tip: Easter Egg Trail takes place 3-6 April 2026

activities.title: Alice in Wonderland
activities.description: A theatre show of Alice in Wonderland
activities.whyItWorks: A family show, good for all ages

transport.fromHackney: Take the Hammersmith & City Line to Hammersmith
transport.tip: Check the show times to ensure a good viewing

activities.title: Peppa Pig's egg-stra special Easter Afternoon Tea Bus Tour
activities.description: Peppa Pig themed afternoon tea will be run with special Easter travel cup and Easter Egg hunt activity on a London bus
activities.whyItWorks: A Peppa Pig-themed bus tour

transport.fromHackney: Varies depending on the route
transport.tip: Read my full review of the Peppa Pig afternoon tea bus here
